entry.title: leetcode(4) sort
entry.content:
215 Kth Largest Element in an Array（medium）

Problem description:
**Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array. Note that it is the kth largest element in the sorted order, not the kth distinct element.**
Thinking:
This problem is easy. We can just use quick sort and get the n-k element.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int findKthLargest(int[] nums, int k) {
        int n = nums.length;
        quickSort(nums,0,n);
        return nums[n-k];
    }

    public void quickSort(int[] nums, int l, int r){
        if(l+1 >= r) return;
        int first = l, last = r-1, key = nums[first];
        while(first < last){
            while(first < last&&nums[last] >= key) last--;
            nums[first] = nums[last];
            while(first < last&&nums[first] <= key) first++;
            nums[last] = nums[first];
        }
        nums[first] = key;
        quickSort(nums,l,first);
        quickSort(nums,first+1,r);
    }
}

347 Top K Frequent Elements（medium）

Problem description:
**Given a non-empty array of integers, return the k most frequent elements.**
Thinking:
We can use May to solve this problem.Map to store elements of array nums. Then
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int[] topKFrequent(int[] nums, int k) { 
        Map map = new HashMap<>();
        for (int num : nums) {
            map.put(num, map.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
        }
        LinkedList[] list = new LinkedList[nums.length + 1];
        for (int key : map.keySet()) {
            int val = map.get(key);
            if (list[val] == null) list[val] = new LinkedList<>();
            list[val].add(key);
        }
        int[] ans = new int[k];
        k--;
        for (int i = nums.length; i > 0 && k > -1; i--) {
            while (list[i] != null && !list[i].isEmpty()) {
                ans[k--] = (int)list[i].poll();
            }
        }
        return ans;
    }
}

451 Sort Characters By Frequency（medium）

Problem description:
**Given a string, sort it in decreasing order based on the frequency of characters.**
Thinking:
This problem is similar to 347 and we use the same thinking to solve it. First, we use Hashmap to get frequency of every letter. Then, use Collections.sort to sort the list according to the frequency with ascending order. Then get value of the element in the list and add it to the null string s. return s.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public String frequencySort(String s) {
        HashMap map = new HashMap<>();
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            map.put(c, map.getOrDefault(c, 0)+1);
        }
        List> list = new ArrayList<>(map.entrySet());
        Collections.sort(list, new Comparator>() {
          public int compare(Map.Entry a, Map.Entry b){
            return b.getValue() - a.getValue();
          }});

        String res = "";
        for (Map.Entry e: list) {
            int x = e.getValue();
            char c = e.getKey();
            for (int i = 0; i < x; i++) {
                res += c;
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
}

75.Sort Colors(mdeium)

Problem description:
**Given an array with n objects colored red, white or blue, sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, with the colors in the order red, white and blue.Here, we will use the integers 0, 1, and 2 to represent the color red, white, and blue respectively. Note: You are not suppose to use the library's sort function for this problem.**
Thinking:
We use 2 pointers, head = 0 and tail = length-1. Then, for loop with k, if nums[k] equals 0(red), then change the value of nums[k] and nums[head]. head ++. if nums[k] equals 2(white), then change the value of nums[k] and nums[tail].And tail--,k--.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public void sortColors(int[] nums) {
        int i = 0;
        int j = nums.length-1;
        for (int k = 0;k <= j;k++){
            if (nums[k] == 0) {
                int temp = nums[k];
                nums[k] = nums[i];
                nums[i] = temp;
                i++;
            } else if (nums[k] == 2) {
                int temp = nums[k];
                nums[k] = nums[j];
                nums[j] = temp;
                j--;
                k--;
            }
        }
    }
}

entry.title: leetcode(3) dichotomy
entry.content:
69 Sqrt(x) (easy)

Problem description:
**Implement int sqrt(int x).Compute and return the square root of x, where x is guaranteed to be a non-negative integer.Since the return type is an integer, the decimal digits are truncated and only the integer part of the result is returned.**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int mySqrt(int x) {
        if(x==0) return x;
        int l=1,r=x, mid, sqrt;
        while(l <= r){
            mid = l + (r-l)/2;
            sqrt = x/mid;
            if(sqrt == mid) {
                return mid;
            }
            else if(mid > sqrt){
                r = mid - 1;
            }else{
                l = mid + 1;
            }
        }
        return r;
    }
}

34 Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array(medium)

Problem description:
**Given an array of integers nums sorted in ascending order, find the starting and ending position of a given target value.Your algorithm's runtime complexity must be in the order of O(log n).If the target is not found in the array, return [-1, -1].**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int[] searchRange(int[] nums, int target) {
        int[] N = {-1,-1};
        if(nums.length == 0) return N;
        int lower = lower_bound(nums, target);
        int upper = upper_bound(nums, target)-1;
        if(lower == nums.length || nums[lower]!= target){
            return N;
        }
        int [] R = {lower,upper};
        return R;
    }

    public int lower_bound(int[] nums, int target){
        int l = 0, r = nums.length, mid;
        while(l < r){
            mid = (r+l)/2;
            if(nums[mid] >= target){
                r = mid;
            }else {
                l = mid + 1;
            }
        }
        return l;
    }
    public int upper_bound(int[] nums, int target){
        int l = 0, r = nums.length, mid;
        while(l < r){
            mid = (r+l)/2;
            if(nums[mid] > target){
                r = mid;
            }else {
                l = mid + 1;
            }
        }
        return l;
    }
}

81 Search in Rotated Sorted Array II(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose an array sorted in ascending order is rotated at some pivot unknown to you beforehand.(i.e., [0,0,1,2,2,5,6] might become [2,5,6,0,0,1,2]).You are given a target value to search. If found in the array return true, otherwise return false.**
Thinking:
We can use start, end, mid pointers to indicate the array. If nums[mid]Solution: 
class Solution {
    public boolean search(int[] nums, int target) {
        int start = 0, end = nums.length-1;
        while(start <= end){
            int mid = (start + end)/2;
            if(nums[mid] == target){
                return true;
            }
            if(nums[start] == nums[mid]){
                start++;
            }else if(nums[mid] <= nums[end]){
                if(target > nums[mid] && target <= nums[end]){
                    start = mid + 1;
                }else {
                    end = mid - 1;
                }
            }else{
                if(target >= nums[start]&& target < nums[mid]){
                    end = mid - 1;
                }else{
                    start = mid + 1;
                }
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
}

154 Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array II(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose an array sorted in ascending order is rotated at some pivot unknown to you beforehand.(i.e., [0,1,2,4,5,6,7] might become [4,5,6,7,0,1,2]).Find the minimum element.The array may contain duplicates.**
Thinking:
We need to use left and right pointers and get mid pointer. The mid pointer has 3 situation:1. In the right part; 2. In the left part; 3. In the boundaries. We use if and else to deal with different situations.
Solution: 
class Solution {
     public int findMin(int[] nums) {
        int l = 0;
        int r = nums.length-1;
        if (nums[r]>nums[l]) return nums[l];
        while (lnums[r] && nums[mid]>=nums[l]) l = mid;
            else if (nums[mid] == nums[r] && nums[mid] == nums[l]) {
                int save = mid;
                while (midl && nums[save]>=nums[save-1]) save--;
                if (save>l) {
                    return nums[save];
                }
                if (save == l && mid == r) return nums[r];
            }
        }
        return nums[r];
    }
}

540 Single Element in a Sorted Array(medium)

Problem description:
**You are given a sorted array consisting of only integers where every element appears exactly twice, except for one element which appears exactly once. Find this single element that appears only once.Follow up: Your solution should run in O(log n) time and O(1) space.**
Thinking:
We use XOR to solve this problem. It is efficient. When previous and behind elements are the same, and x^b = 0. Then, a != b, x^b = b. Get the single one x = b, and b is less than the later elements and x can keep its value.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int singleNonDuplicate(int[] nums) {           
        int x = nums[0]; 
        for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++){   
            x^=nums[i]; 
        } 
        return x;
    }
}

4 Median of Two Sorted Arrays(hard)

Problem description:
**There are two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively.Find the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).You may assume nums1 and nums2 cannot be both empty.**
Thinking:
This problem is not hard. We can merge two sorted arrays and get the mid value in the middle position.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public double findMedianSortedArrays(int[] nums1, int[] nums2) {
        int n = nums1.length, m = nums2.length;
        int l = m + n;
        int [] nums3 = new int [l];
        int i = 0, j = 0, k = 0;
        while(k < l){
            if(i>=n||j>=m) break; 
            if(nums1[i] < nums2[j]){ 
                nums3[k] = nums1[i];
                i++;
            }else{
                nums3[k] = nums2[j];
                j++;
            }
           k++; 
        }
        if(i>=n) {
            while(k < l) {
                nums3[k] = nums2[j];
                j++;
                k++;
            }
        }else{
            while(k < l) {
                nums3[k] = nums1[i];
                i++;
                k++;
            }
        }
        double a = nums3[(l-1)/2];
        double b = nums3[l/2];
        return (a+b)/2;
    }
}

entry.title: 每日一曲 Dreams
entry.content:
去年的秋天，我听着这首小红莓的Dreams,漫步在广州上下九的街道上。那天，担忧已久的签证面试直接issued了，签证官对我说：“Have a nice trip.”这是一种从没有过的感觉，我终于有机会看看太平洋彼岸的世界。那里不是天堂，也不是地狱，是一个陌生的地方，我将在那里独自生活，一切从零开始。过去几年的迷茫，失望，惋惜仿佛都不复存在，因为从零开始已经没有什么可以失去了。广州的天气正好，我吃着老字号的双皮奶，艇仔粥，向朋友分享着好消息。 Dolores的声音实在太好听，有一种空灵而迷幻的感觉。王菲早年正是模仿她的唱腔走红，但两者又不是同一种感觉。最早知道Cranberries是因为Zombies anti-war的歌，里面Dolores的声音非常坚定，强大，以至于我一开始没有意识到这两首歌是同一个人唱的。
美好的午后过去后，那种喜悦的心情也不见了。在傍晚坐汽车回家，开始担心自己的未来学业，懊悔这几个月因为担心签证而没有好好利用的时光。人啊，真的太难获得一些真正身心愉悦的时刻了。

entry.title: 每日一曲-Mother
entry.content: Every time I heard this song, I feel depressed. This is not feigned sadness to pretend I am unique. The lyric and melody really go into my heart. When you understand what this song is singing, you will cry. Not so many people understand the feeling of having such a mother. Mother or father, whatever, control everything of you. Your study, your interest, your girl friend, your living and even your mind. Mother will never leave you alone no matter how wishful you are. You have original sin because you were born in this family. I don't know, how long it takes for us to escape from such a mother. I feel scaring because what mother will do if she is going to lose her power. Many children will suffer for this and this is inevitable.

entry.title: leetcode(1) Greedy
entry.content:
Foreword

This series of articles are written for sovling problems in leetcode.

455 Assign Cookies (easy)

Problem description:
**Assume you are an awesome parent and want to give your children some cookies. But, you should give each child at most one cookie. Each child i has a greed factor gi, which is the minimum size of a cookie that the child will be content with; and each cookie j has a size sj. If sj >= gi, we can assign the cookie j to the child i, and the child i will be content. Your goal is to maximize the number of your content children and output the maximum number.**
Note: You may assume the greed factor is always positive. You cannot assign more than one cookie to one child.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. We just fulfill the child who has lower greedy factor with the least size cookie which could fulfill this child. Therefore, we need to sort the two arrays first and then it is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int findContentChildren(int[] g, int[] s) {
        Arrays.sort(g);
        Arrays.sort(s);
        int child = 0;
        int cookie = 0;
        while(child< g.length && cookie< s.length){
            if(g[child]<=s[cookie]) child++;
            cookie++;
        }
        return child;
    }
}

135 Candy (hard)

Problem description:
**There are N children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value.You are giving candies to these children subjected to the following requirements: 1.Each child must have at least one candy. 2.Children with a higher rating get more candies than their neighbors. What is the minimum candies you must give?**
Thinking:
This problem is not as diffcult as we imagine. We don't need to sort the array. We can get solution with two traverses. First, set a array for storing candy allocated to children and all set at 1. Then, from left to right, if the child on the right rating higher than the left child, then the candy of the right children should be updated to be the candy of left child plus one. Finally, from right to left, if the child on the left rating higher than the right child and his candy is not more that the left one, then the candy of the left children should be updated to be the candy of right child plus one.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int candy(int[] ratings) {
        int n = ratings.length;
        int [] candy = new int [n];
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) candy[i] = 1;
        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){
            if(ratings[i-1]< ratings[i]) candy[i]=candy[i-1]+1;
        }
        for(int i=0;i< n-1;i++){
            if(ratings[n-i-1]< ratings[n-i-2]) {
                    if(candy[n-i-1]>=candy[n-i-2]) candy[n-i-2] = candy[n-i-1]+1;
                }
              }                 
        int sum = 0;
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) sum = sum + candy[i];
        return sum;
    }
}

435 Non-overlapping Intervals (medium)

Problem description:
**Given a collection of intervals, find the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.**
Thinking:
Using greed pricinple, sort the arrays first according to last element(interval's tail) in ascending sequence. Then picking the interval which has smaller tail as possible as we can, skipping the overlapping intervals for the chosen intervals.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int eraseOverlapIntervals(int[][] intervals) {
        int n = intervals.length;
        if(n==0) return 0;
        int [] tail = new int [n];
        int [] head = new int [n];
        int [] flag = new int [n];
        Arrays.sort(intervals,new Comparator< int[]>(){
           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){
                  return a[1]-b[1];
           }
        });
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) {
            tail[i] = intervals[i][1];
            head[i] = intervals[i][0];
            flag[i] = 0;
        }
        flag[0] = 1;
        int t = tail[0];
        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){
            if(head[i]>=t) {
                flag[i] = 1;
                t = tail[i];
            }
        }
        int sum=0;
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) sum+=flag[i];
        return n-sum;
    }
}

605 Can Place Flowers(easy)

Problem description:
**Suppose you have a long flowerbed in which some of the plots are planted and some are not. However, flowers cannot be planted in adjacent plots - they would compete for water and both would die. Given a flowerbed (represented as an array containing 0 and 1, where 0 means empty and 1 means not empty), and a number n, return if n new flowers can be planted in it without violating the no-adjacent-flowers rule.**
Thinking:
This problem is easy. Using if to judge a place and its neighbors are emtpy. And consider extreme situation like size = 1.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public boolean canPlaceFlowers(int[] flowerbed, int n) {
        int len = flowerbed.length;
        int count = 0;
        if(len==1){
            if(flowerbed[0]==0) count++;
        }
        else {
            if(flowerbed[0]==0&&flowerbed[1]==0){
                count++;
                flowerbed[0] = 1;
            }
            for(int i=1;i< len-1;i++){
                if(flowerbed[i-1]==0&&flowerbed[i]==0&&flowerbed[i+1]==0){
                    count++;
                    flowerbed[i] = 1;
                }
            }
            if(flowerbed[len-1]==0&&flowerbed[len-2]==0){
                count++;
                flowerbed[len-1] = 1;
            }
        }
        if(count>=n) return true;
        else return false;
    }
}

452 Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons(medium)

Problem description:
**There are a number of spherical balloons spread in two-dimensional space. For each balloon, provided input is the start and end coordinates of the horizontal diameter. Since it's horizontal, y-coordinates don't matter and hence the x-coordinates of start and end of the diameter suffice. Start is always smaller than end. There will be at most 104 balloons. An arrow can be shot up exactly vertically from different points along the x-axis. A balloon with xstart and xend bursts by an arrow shot at x if xstart ≤ x ≤ xend. There is no limit to the number of arrows that can be shot. An arrow once shot keeps travelling up infinitely. The problem is to find the minimum number of arrows that must be shot to burst all balloons.**
Thinking:
This problem is similar to 435. Using Array.sort()first according to the second element of the arrays and judging overlapping. We do not add the count until the overlapping does not happen in this round.By the way, remember to update the previous comparing val.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int findMinArrowShots(int[][] points) {
        int n = points.length;
        if(n==0) return 0;
        int [] tail = new int [n];
        int [] head = new int [n];
        int flag = 0;
        Arrays.sort(points,new Comparator< int[]>(){
           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){
                  return a[1]-b[1];
           }
        });
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) {
            tail[i] = points[i][1];
            head[i] = points[i][0];
        }
        int pre = tail[0];
        flag++;
        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){
            if(head[i]>pre){
                flag++;
                pre = tail[i];
            }
        }
        return flag;
    }
}

763 Partition Labels(medium)

Problem description:
**A string S of lowercase English letters is given. We want to partition this string into as many parts as possible so that each letter appears in at most one part, and return a list of integers representing the size of these parts.**
Thinking:
This problem is kind of complicated.If we do same pre-processing, the problem will become simple. First, we need to get the starting location and ending location of all of the letters, using HashMap containor. Then, we use the similar pattern, considering the same letters as a box with length(end minus start). Then, if the next letter(supposed to be different from the previous one)'s head is bigger than the previous one's tail, then they can be in different part or they must be in the same part.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public List partitionLabels(String S) {
        //get frequency, location of all elements
        ArrayList loation = new ArrayList();
        Map countNum = new HashMap(); 
        Map charLocationMap = new HashMap(); 
        char temp;
        char[] tempList=S.toCharArray();
        for (int i = 0; i < tempList.length; i++) 
        {
             temp = tempList[i];
             if(charLocationMap.get(temp)==null)
             {
                 ArrayList charLocList = new ArrayList();
                 charLocList.add(i);
                 charLocationMap.put(temp,charLocList);
             }
             else
             {
                 List< Integer > arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);
                 arrList.add(i);
                 charLocationMap.put(temp,arrList);
             }
        }
        ArrayList NumberCount = new ArrayList(charLocationMap.keySet());
        for (int i = 0; i < NumberCount.size(); i++)
        {
            char tem = (char)NumberCount.get(i);
            List arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(tem);
        }
        //get partition
        int len = S.length();
        int [] flag = new int [len];
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) flag[i] = 0;
        temp = tempList[0];
        List arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);
        int n = arrList.size();
        int tail = arrList.get(n-1);
        int head = arrList.get(0); 
        for (int i = 1; i < tempList.length; i++) {
            temp = tempList[i];
            arrList = (ArrayList)charLocationMap.get(temp);
            n = arrList.size();
            head = arrList.get(0);
            if(head> tail) {
                flag[tail] = 1;
                tail = arrList.get(n-1);
            }
            else{
                if(arrList.get(n-1)> tail) tail = arrList.get(n-1);
            }
        }
        flag[len-1] = 1;
        List lst = new ArrayList ();
        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) {
            if(flag[i]==0) count++;
            else{
                lst.add(count+1);
                count = 0;
            }
        }
        return lst;
    }
}

122 Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II（easy）

Problem description:
**Say you have an array prices for which the ith element is the price of a given stock on day i.Design an algorithm to find the maximum profit. You may complete as many transactions as you like (i.e., buy one and sell one share of the stock multiple times).**
Note: You may not engage in multiple transactions at the same time (i.e., you must sell the stock before you buy again).
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int maxProfit(int[] prices) {
        int n = prices.length;
        if(n==1) return 0;
        int profit = 0;
        int today;
        int tomorrow;
        for(int i=0;i< n-1;i++){
            today = prices[i];
            tomorrow = prices[i+1];
            if(tomorrow>today) profit = profit + tomorrow - today;
        }
        return profit;
    }
}

406 Queue Reconstruction by Height(medium)

Problem description:
**Suppose you have a random list of people standing in a queue. Each person is described by a pair of integers (h, k), where h is the height of the person and k is the number of people in front of this person who have a height greater than or equal to h. Write an algorithm to reconstruct the queue.**
Note: The number of people is less than 1,100.
Thinking:
This problem need sort and insert. First, sort the 2-dimensional array according to the first value(height) from high to short. If their height are the same, comparing the second values, from small fo big. After that, we build a new array, and insert the array according to the second element.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int[][] reconstructQueue(int[][] people) {
        Arrays.sort(people,new Comparator< int[]>(){
           public int compare(int[] a,int[] b){
                if(a[0] == b[0]) return a[1]-b[1];
                else{
                    return b[0]-a[0];
                }
           }
        });
        int n = people.length;
        int [][] a = new int [n][2];
        List list = new ArrayList ();
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++) list.add(people[i]);
        for(int i=0;i< n;i++){
            int[] b = list.remove(i);
            int p = b[1];
            list.add(p,b);
        }
        list.toArray(a);
        return a;
    }
}

665 Non-decreasing Array（easy）

Problem description:
**Given an array nums with n integers, your task is to check if it could become non-decreasing by modifying at most 1 element.We define an array is non-decreasing if nums[i] <= nums[i + 1] holds for every i (0-based) such that (0 <= i <= n - 2).**
Thinking:
This problem is easy.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public boolean checkPossibility(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        boolean b = true;
        int count = 0;
        for(int i=1;i< n;i++){
            if(nums[i-1]>nums[i]) count++;
            if(count >= 1){
                if(count >1) {
                    b = false;
                    break;
                }
                else{
                    if(nums[i-1]>nums[i]){
                        if(i>1&&i< n-1){
                            if((nums[i-2]> nums[i])&&(nums[i-1]> nums[i+1])) {
                                b = false;
                                break;
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            } 
        }
        return b;
    }
}

entry.title: leetcode(2) Pointer
entry.content:
455 Two Sum II - Input array is sorted (easy)

Problem description:
**Given an array of integers that is already sorted in ascending order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number.The function twoSum should return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to the target, where index1 must be less than index2.**
Note: Your returned answers (both index1 and index2) are not zero-based. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution and you may not use the same element twice.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. The given array has been sorted in ascending order. Therefore, we use two indices in starting and ending postion to search. Summating numbers[index1] and numbers[index2] and comparing to target. If sum > target, index2 minus 1, or index1 plus 1.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public int[] twoSum(int[] numbers, int target) {
        int n = numbers.length;
        int p = 0;
        int q = n-1;
        int sum = numbers[p]+numbers[q];
        while(sum!=target){
            if(sum>target) q--;
            else p++;
            sum = numbers[p]+numbers[q];
        }
        int [] a = new int[2];
        a[0] = p+1;
        a[1] = q+1;
        return a;
    }
}

88 Merge Sorted Array(easy)

Problem description:
**Given two sorted integer arrays nums1 and nums2, merge nums2 into nums1 as one sorted array.**
Note: The number of elements initialized in nums1 and nums2 are m and n respectively. You may assume that nums1 has enough space (size that is greater or equal to m + n) to hold additional elements from nums2.
Thinking:
This problem is easy. USing three pointers to finish. One for nums3 which clones nums1, one for nums2 and one for nums1. Nums1 has to be adjust according to the problem.
Solution: 
class Solution {
    public void merge(int[] nums1, int m, int[] nums2, int n) {
        int [] nums3 = new int [m];
        int p = 0;
        int q = 0;
        int pq = 0;
        for(int i=0;i< m;i++) nums3[i] = nums1[i];
        while(p


142 Linked List Cycle II(Medium)

Problem description:

**Given a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null.To represent a cycle in the given linked list, we use an integer pos which represents the position (0-indexed) in the linked list where tail connects to. If pos is -1, then there is no cycle in the linked list.**

Note:
Do not modify the linked list.

Thinking:

Using Folyd method to judge whether there is a cycle. Set two pointer, fast(2 step) and slow(1 step). If there is no cycle, fast pointer will reach the end. Otherwise, fast and slow pinters will meet in one node, then update fast pointer as head, and update it with 1 step. Finally, fast and slow will meet again in the start node of the cycle.

Solution: 
 


public class Solution {
    public ListNode detectCycle(ListNode head) {
        if(head==null || head.next==null) return null;
        ListNode temp=head,fast=head;
        while(fast!=null && fast.next!=null){
            temp=temp.next;
            if(fast.next!=null){
                fast=fast.next.next;
            }else{
                return null;
            }

            if(temp==fast){
               temp=head;
                while(temp!=fast){
                    temp=temp.next;
                    fast=fast.next;
                }
                return temp;
            }
        }
        return null;
    }
}




76 Minimum Window Substring(Hard)

Problem description:

**Given a string S and a string T, find the minimum window in S which will contain all the characters in T in complexity O(n).**

Thinking:

 This problem we use C++. First we need to get information about letters in string s and t, storing in a vector and vector. Then, use left and right pointer to search. r iterates first, when passes through all the letters in string t, record the length of substring.then, move left pointer, when minus a letter in string t, then we need to find one by using right pointer. 

Solution: 
 


class Solution {
public:
    string minWindow(string s, string t) {
        vector chars(128,0);
        vector flag(128,false);
        for(int i=0;i< t.size();++i){
            flag[t[i]] = true;
            ++chars[t[i]];
        }
        int cnt = 0, l = 0, min_l = 0, min_size = s.size()+1;
        for(int r = 0; r < s.size();++r){
            if(flag[s[r]]){
                if(--chars[s[r]] >= 0){
                    ++cnt;
                }
            }
            while(cnt == t.size()){
                if(r-l+1 < min_size){
                    min_l =l;
                    min_size = r-l+1;
                }
                if(flag[s[l]]&&++chars[s[l0){
                    --cnt;
                }
                ++l;
            }
        }
        return min_size > s.size()? "": s.substr(min_l,min_size); 
    }
};



633 Minimum Window Substring(easy)
Problem description:

**Given a non-negative integer c, your task is to decide whether there're two integers a and b such that a2 + b2 = c.**

Thinking:

 This problem is similar to the two sum problem. We can use two pointers to iterate until they meet. 

Solution: 
 


class Solution {
    public boolean judgeSquareSum(int c) {
        double C = Double.valueOf(c);
        double sq = Math.sqrt(C);
        int q = new Double(sq).intValue();
        int p = 0;
        int sum = q*q+p*p;
        while(sum!=c&&p<=q){
            if(sum>c) q--;
            else p++;
            sum = q*q+p*p;
            if(sum == c) break;
        }
        if(p<=q) return true;
        else return false;
    }
}



680 Valid Palindrome II(easy)
Problem description:

**Given a non-empty string s, you may delete at most one character. Judge whether you can make it a palindrome.**

Thinking:

 This problem is similar to the two sum problem. We can use two pointers to iterate until they meet. Comparing the two chars, if they are not equal, judge whether the substring is Palindrome except s[p] or s[q]. We build a new function, isPalindrome(String s, int Charposit);

Solution: 
 


class Solution {
    public boolean validPalindrome(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int p = 0;
        int q = n-1;
        int flag = 0;
        while(p<=q){
            if(s.charAt(p)==s.charAt(q)){
                p++;
                q--;
                continue;
            }
            else{  
                return isPalindrome(s,p)||isPalindrome(s,q);
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    private boolean isPalindrome(String s, int charToSkip){
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("");
        int l  = 0;
        int r = s.length()-1;
        while(l<=r){
            if(l==charToSkip)l++;
            else if(r==charToSkip)r--;
            else{
                if(s.charAt(l)!=s.charAt(r))return false;
                l++;r--;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }
}



524 Longest Word in Dictionary through Deleting(medium)
Problem description:

**Given a string and a string dictionary, find the longest string in the dictionary that can be formed by deleting some characters of the given string. If there are more than one possible results, return the longest word with the smallest lexicographical order. If there is no possible result, return the empty string.**

Thinking:

 

Solution: 
 


class Solution {
        public String findLongestWord(String s, List d) {
        int max = 0;
        String result = "";
        if (s.length() == 0) {
            return result;
        }
        for (String target : d) {
            if (target.length() == 0) {
                continue;
            }
            int s_idx = 0;
            int d_idx = 0;
           while (d_idx < target.length()) {
                if (s.charAt(s_idx) == target.charAt(d_idx)) {
                    s_idx++;
                    d_idx++;
                }
                else {
                    s_idx++;
                }

                if (s_idx == s.length()) {
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (d_idx < target.length()) {
                continue;
            }
            else {
                if (target.length() > max) {
                    result = target;
                    max = target.length();
                }
                else if (target.length() == max) {
                    result = target.compareTo(result)>0? result:target;
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
}
]]>
